rationale.bug_summary: OMSConsumeCE, OMSConsumeOB, and OMSConsumePending lack ~oms_muted guard. After OMSRecover sets muted=TRUE, all three actions remain enabled and can advance cursors and write to oms_log. OMSViewChange subsequently wipes oms_log and recomputes catch-up from the already-advanced cursor, permanently losing any consumed effects that ArchiverIngestOMS had not yet ingested.
rationale.reviewer_objection_assessed: Reviewer correctly notes the proposal slightly overstates severity by claiming a property violation when no explicit OMS archival completeness invariant exists in the spec. This is accurate. The consequence is semantic incorrectness and a potential archiver gap, not a violation of any numbered invariant. Severity is medium.
rationale.fix_correctness: The fix is minimal and correct. Adding ~oms_muted[p] as a precondition guard to all three consumption actions blocks consumption during the recovery window. OMSViewChange is the designed catch-up path during muted periods and does not write to oms_log. No liveness risk: WF on OMSUnmute and archiver ingest actions ensures the muted period terminates and consumption resumes.
rationale.state_space_impact: reduces: three actions disabled during muted period, fewer interleaving states during recovery
rationale.properties_affected_by_fix: none. Fix is purely additive precondition. INV18, INV11, INV8 unaffected per both proposer and reviewer analysis.

dispositive_points: The trace is valid and confirmed by reviewer: OMSRecover at line 562-568 sets alive=TRUE, muted=TRUE with cursors unchanged. Consumption then fires on alive check alone.
OMSViewChange cursor computation at line 516 uses the post-consumption cursor as its starting offset, meaning effects consumed-but-wiped are unreachable in the new session.
WF(ArchiverIngestOMS) guarantees eventual ingestion but not immediate ingestion, so the race window is reachable in any finite model.
No dispositive conflict between proposal and review. Reviewer accepts the fix, objects only to severity framing. Severity framing does not affect correctness of the fix.
verdict_note: Reviewer's overstatement objection is noted but does not change the ruling. The fix is correct regardless of whether a formal invariant captures the consequence. Muted OMS should not consume; the spec should enforce this.
